notes: As in the measurement of the outer porch he had pointed to Solomon's temple, so here in the edifice itself, he points to the old tabernacle, which being eight boards in breadth (each one and a half cubits broad) would make in all twelve cubits, as here. On the interior it was only ten cubits.
before the temple [that is, before, or in front of the most holy place]
over against the door, cieled with wood round about, and from the ground up to the windows, and the windows [were] covered;

two faces - could see past and future - the Hebrew measurement of time 'aleph-tav' The face of the man for the premortal or past and the lion for the Millennial or the future. The palm tree is to be erect or straight. We standing in time, between the past and the future, we need to stand erect or faithful in our time.
[were] made upon the walls; and [there were] thick planks upon the face of the porch without.
Eze 42:1-20. CHAMBERS OF THE PRIESTS: MEASUREMENTS OF THE TEMPLE
